text.title: Timeline Lines Must Cite a Signal Source
text.summary: Each timeline entry carries a citation to a structured signal; uncited lines are forbidden.

section.title: Closed signal set with required citation IDs
section.p: The drafting agent receives only a closed signal set: `slack_messages[]` (with ts + author), `alerts[]` (with fired_at + alert_id), `deploys[]` (with sha + service + ts), `action_items[]` (from `/incident actionitem` invocations), `linked_prs[]`. Each timeline line in the draft MUST cite at least one source by ID. Lines with no citation are invalid; lines paraphrasing a Slack message must include the message link.

rule.statement: Every entry in the postmortem `Timeline` section MUST end with a bracketed citation list referencing at least one signal ID (`[slack:T123/p98...]`, `[alert:HighDBLatency@14:02]`, `[deploy:abc123]`). The agent MUST refuse to emit timeline lines without a citation, and MUST NOT cite signals outside the supplied closed set.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated timeline entries are the most damaging output of an LLM-drafted postmortem because timestamps look authoritative. Forcing a citation per line makes hallucinations syntactically detectable: a non-existent ID fails a post-draft validator before the human ever reads it.

example.code:
## Timeline (UTC)
- 14:02 deploy `abc123` reduced pool size 20 → 5 [deploy:abc123] - 14:04 alert `HighDBLatency` fired (p99 = 1.2s) [alert:HighDBLatency@14:04] - 14:06 @alice posted "pool exhausted?" [slack:T123/p9806120] - 14:11 manual revert merged [pr:#4581][slack:T123/p9806840]
example.note: Every line is independently auditable; a validator can resolve every citation.

example.code: - 14:02 a recent change shrank the connection pool - around 14:05 the database started timing out - shortly after, the team noticed and rolled back
example.note: No citations, vague timestamps, paraphrased intent. Nothing prevents the model from inventing the second line.

antipattern.description: Allowing the agent to cite a "general knowledge" source like "AWS docs" for a specific incident timeline.
antipattern.reason: The signal set is the universe of truth for one incident; an outside reference smuggles assumptions about what happened from training data, not from the actual outage.

section.reference: Prompt fragment enforcing the citation grammar.
